data.notice_text:
REQUEST FOR EXPRESSIONS OF INTEREST

 (INDIVIDUAL CONSULTING SERVICES)

Country: Republic of Uzbekistan

Implementing Agency: National Agency for Social Protection (NASP) under the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan.

Project Name: Innovative Social Protection System for Inclusion of Vulnerable People (INSON)

Financing Agreement No. 75550-UZ / Grant Agreement No. TF C4589-UZ

Assignment: Expert on establishment of small-scale family homes for persons in need of care

Package number (as per Procurement Plan): UZ-INSON-CS-IC-2.1.10.2.

 

The Republic of Uzbekistan has received financing from the World Bank toward the implementation of the Innovative Social Protection System for Inclusion of Vulnerable People (INSON) Project and intends to apply part of the proceeds for consulting services.

The consulting services include provision of expert support to the National Agency for Social Protection under the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan in the development and implementation of a model of small-scale family homes for persons in need of long-term care or support, as an alternative to large institutional facilities. This model provides for accommodation of 6-8 persons in conditions close to a family environment, ensuring social support, safety, autonomy, social integration and a decent standard of living.

The Consultant will provide expert support in the following areas: analysis of the current legislation, procedures and practices for placement of persons in need of long-term care or support; development of a national concept and standards for small-scale family homes; preparation of assessment criteria and placement mechanisms; development of service delivery standards and operational procedures; preparation of methodological materials, allocation of roles and responsibilities, checklists and referral algorithms; development of a staff training program; support for pilot implementation of the model in Tashkent, Andijan and Fergana regions; development of a monitoring system, quality assessment and key performance indicators; preparation of proposals and materials for the development of regulatory documents.

The estimated level of effort is up to 60 working days over a period of 6 months. Of these, 5 working days are envisaged for conducting online training for 300-400 staff members, while the remaining days are for supporting the practical implementation of pilot projects in the selected regions. The expected date of commencement of the assignment is July, 2026.

The detailed Terms of Reference (TOR) can be found at the following link:

https://docs.google.com/document/d/1jL4Y9wOv6qXY1Iuv6mkHDmZ_2nEiV7mE/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=104755491226149341341&rtpof=true&sd=true

The National Agency for Social Protection (NASP) under the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan invites qualified individuals (“Consultants”) to express their interest in providing the Services. Interested Consultants should provide information demonstrating that they have the required qualifications and relevant experience to perform the Services (attach a CV describing experience in similar positions, under similar conditions, etc.). Employees of firms may express interest in performing the assignment through their employing firm, in which case only the experience and qualifications of the candidates will be taken into account in the selection process.

Consultant Selection Criteria:

Education and academic qualification: higher education in social policy, social work, public administration, gerontology, disability studies or another related field. Additional specialized training in social protection, long-term care, deinstitutionalization or community-based care is an advantage.

Professional experience: at least 8 years of relevant professional experience in social or municipal assistance, social protection, case management and/or inter-agency coordination. Experience working with persons in need of long-term care or support, lonely and vulnerable persons is an advantage.

Experience in developing regulatory, methodological and operational documents: experience in developing and implementing regulatory, methodological and operational documents, including service standards, standard operating procedures, allocation of roles and responsibilities, methodological guidelines, checklists, classifiers, assessment and referral forms. Experience in developing documents for social services, small-scale family homes, group homes, assisted living or similar models is a particular advantage.

Experience in developing a service model, assessment criteria and placement mechanisms: experience in developing or applying methodologies for social/municipal assistance, needs assessment criteria, referral and placement mechanisms, assessment of functional independence, social and psychological assessment. Experience in building a service chain is an advantage: identification, registration, assessment, placement, service provision and monitoring.

Experience in training, implementation and inter-agency coordination: experience in developing and delivering training programs, including training of trainers, preparation of training materials, practical manuals and instructions for social workers and other specialists. Experience in inter-agency work with government bodies, medical institutions, local authorities, non-governmental non-profit organizations and service providers, as well as experience in pilot implementation of new models of social services, is an advantage.

Language skills: excellent command of Russian. Knowledge of Uzbek is an advantage.

The attention of interested consultants (including firms) is drawn to paragraphs 3.14, 3.16 and 3.17 of the World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers dated September 2023 (“Regulations”), setting forth the World Bank’s policy on conflict of interest.

Further information can be obtained at the address below during office hours from 09:00 to 18:00 (Tashkent time).

Expressions of interest, supported by a CV, must be delivered in written form to the address below (in person, by mail or by e-mail) by July 10, 2026.

 

INSON PIU under NASP

Attention: Mr. Mirodil Khusanov - Project Coordinator.

Address: 3 Amir Temur Street, Tashkent, Uzbekistan, 100060

Tel.: +998712395976.

E-mail: piuinsonprocurement@gmail.com, cc: s.maxmudov@ihma.uz
